item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The absurdity of it, followed by the potential at the end for this to go off the rails because of the Foundation's interference clinched it for me. You *know* one of the witnesses is going to eat a goldfish, and then there's a spread of the memory. Soon, goodbye to all of the goldfish!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Honestly, I really like it. I find it funny and absurd, and I like that you actually included Recovery, which I haven't seen a lot in series IV. Even though it isn't so deep, the idea is well-explored and well-written. I like funny skips in the mainlist. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I feel the article relies too heavily on the comical concept of eating your friends pets to make it interesting. It seems comedy is being supplemented as actual substance. It was well written, but I feel it lacks depth.

This is by no means a bad thing, I actually chuckled at the comical aspects of the article. Did you ever consider making this an SCP - J ?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is it sad that I know the shitpost the title is referring too?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Shared memories and eating pets are the same thing out-of-universe too. There isn't a single difference. Plausibility (as a concept) only really applies to hypotheticals, while this is established fact.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You're welcome. I believe that I understand that what you are saying is an in-universe fact of this skip, but I just can't bring the same logic into the realm of plausibility.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Who said he had a choice in the matter?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why was Pitt willing to participate in this? Did he buy a goldfish just so this would happen?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Eating a pet is exactly like having a shared memory. There isn't a connection because you can't connect something to itself. Thank's for the feedback.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This reads like a bit of a MadLibs skip, to me. Those don't automatically bother me, as long as some of the randomly-chosen elements are connected in some way. This is missing that connection between swallowing fish and shared memories, in my opinion. -1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the way this comes sort of full circle.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is definitely better than the previous iteration, especially the tidbit in the addendum about wanting to swallow goldfish. +1

item.title: reposting
item.content:encoded:
the old one got mixed reviews so i upgraded it a bit, moved information around and added new content

thanks to wjs, rioghail, and i think somebody else for on-site feedback

i still don't know why i wrote this or how i came up with it
